title: Simple Landing
userRequest: Create a clean single-page website for a neighborhood coffee shop called Northline Coffee. Include a hero section, short about section, featured drinks, store hours, location details, and a clear call to action to visit the shop. Make it feel warm, modern, and easy to read on mobile.

title: Personal Portfolio
userRequest: Build a personal portfolio website for a frontend engineer named Mina Park. Include an introduction, selected projects, skills, short bio, testimonials, and a contact section. The design should feel polished and credible without looking like a generic template.

title: SaaS Product Intro
userRequest: Design a homepage for a SaaS product called SprintGrid that helps product teams plan launches and track dependencies. Include a hero, product benefits, feature cards, social proof, pricing preview, and a primary CTA for starting a free trial. Make the hierarchy sharp and conversion-focused.

title: Event Page
userRequest: Create an event website for the 2026 Seoul Design Futures Summit. It should include the event overview, speakers, schedule highlights, venue details, ticket tiers, FAQs, and a strong registration section. The page should feel energetic and premium, with clear scanning for busy visitors.

title: Developer Tool Homepage
userRequest: Build a homepage for a developer tool called TraceMint that helps engineering teams inspect logs, traces, and deploy health in one interface. Include a strong technical hero, feature sections, workflow diagrams or visual explanation areas, pricing, and a docs-oriented secondary CTA. The audience is engineering leaders and developers.

title: Mobile App Promo
userRequest: Create a promotional landing page for a mobile app named HabitLoop that helps users build routines with streak tracking and lightweight coaching. Include app preview visuals, feature highlights, user benefits, app store download CTAs, and a section explaining how the product works. Make it feel lively and mobile-first.

title: Ambiguous Website Ask
userRequest: I need a website for my new thing. It should look modern, help people understand what it does, and make them want to contact me. Keep it professional but not boring.

title: Constraint Heavy
userRequest: Build a website homepage for a legal-tech startup called ClausePilot. Constraints: use only static HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript, no external assets or libraries, no placeholder copy, include responsive behavior for mobile and desktop, keep total delivered files to three or fewer, use a restrained navy-and-sand palette, include hero, trust indicators, product explanation, pricing summary, FAQ, and contact CTA, and make sure the output is implementation-ready.
